attacking_style: Opportunistic sprinter from reduced groups after climbing selections, typically launching 180-200m out on uphill or technical finishes. Excels in breakaway sprints where tactical positioning trumps pure speed.
team_tactics: Relies on minimal leadout support (1-2 riders maximum), often self-positioning in final kilometers. Team sets tempo on climbs to thin field rather than protecting for mass sprints. Functions independently when needed.
terrain_preference: Uphill sprint finishes after prolonged climbs (12-15km) that eliminate pure sprinters but not punchy climbers. Thrives in reduced bunch scenarios where positioning and diesel endurance matter more than explosive watts.
vulnerability: Distance judgment on gradual climbs—repeatedly goes too early (50m+ too far) when leading out own sprint. Exposed in headwind finishes and pure flat sprints against specialist fast-finishers.
key_patterns: Hunts time bonuses aggressively at intermediate sprints and bonus finishes for GC positioning (3 instances)
Launches sprint attacks 180-200m out, often too early on gradual climbs requiring recalibration (4 instances)
Elite breakaway finishing: refuses pulls final kilometer to conserve energy, then outsprints fresher rivals (2 wins)
Survives climbing selections via diesel durability rather than explosive power, fights back when dropped (3 instances)
Tactical maturity in reduced groups: exploits positioning over pure speed when sprinter field thinned (4 instances)
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 resurgence after 2021 crash—Tour de Suisse and Poland wins show full recovery, returning to 2018-2019 breakaway sprint effectiveness
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 5
